original_datasets.name: FINAL demo open fluency
original_datasets.columns: [id, Sample, d_age, d_gender, o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo, vf_an_01 ... vf_an_50, bfasi01 ... bfasi10, bfaso01 ... bfaso10, o01 ... o12, neo_01 ... neo_240, filter_$]

summary_statistics.info: 516 rows; 346 columns; dtypes: float64(4), int64(23), str(319).

original_datasets.notes: Contains demographics, multiple openness measures (NEO-FFI-3, BFAS, NEO-PI-3 totals/items), and 50 verbal fluency response columns (vf_an_01...vf_an_50). Many vf_an_36..vf_an_50 are fully empty; analysis equates nodes across groups and requires minimal preprocessing/cleaning.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the analysis without any restrictions.

analysis_path.path_description: Construct semantic networks for low vs high openness groups from verbal fluency responses; compute ASPL, CC, and Q; compare groups via bootstrapped t-tests.
preprocessing_steps: Import original data from /app/data/FINAL demo open fluency.csv.
Construct latent openness score from available totals (o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo) and median split into low vs high groups.
Clean fluency tokens (lowercase, strip punctuation); drop columns with 100% missing.
Keep only animal responses produced by at least 2 participants in each group (equated nodes).
Build binary word-by-participant matrices per group and compute pairwise word similarity (positive Pearson correlation).
Construct undirected weighted graphs from positive associations (edges).
Case-wise bootstrap (1000 iterations) by resampling participants within each group; recompute networks and metrics each iteration.

key_choices.outcome_measure: Group differences in semantic network metrics: average shortest path length (ASPL), clustering coefficient (CC), and modularity (Q).
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Openness group (high vs low) defined by median split of a latent openness composite from o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Equate nodes across groups to items produced by at least 2 participants in each group.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Impute column means for openness totals when forming the latent composite; ignore missing fluency entries.
key_choices.data_processing: Token cleaning; binary matrices; correlate rows; build graphs; compute ASPL (using inverse weight as distance), CC (weighted), and Q (community modularity).
key_choices.model_choice: Welch two-sample t-test on bootstrapped metric distributions between groups.
key_choices.inference_rule: Two-sided p-value from t-test comparing high vs low groups for each metric.

outcome.name: network_metrics
outcome.definition: ASPL, CC, Q computed from group-level semantic networks built from verbal fluency responses.

main_predictor.name: openness_group
main_predictor.definition: High vs low groups via median split on latent openness composite from available totals (o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo).

analysis_code.notes: Reads /app/data/FINAL demo open fluency.csv; writes /app/data/task1_results.json. Approximates reported R pipeline; uses positive Pearson correlations for edges and python-louvain for modularity if available. TMFG filtering and small-world model tests are not implemented.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path and dataset. Do not change the focal claim or switch datasets.

tasks.task_instruction: In the partial network that you use, the rate of nodes remaining should be 90%.

analysis_path.path_description: Using 90% node-wise partial semantic networks, compare ASPL between high vs low openness groups via bootstrapped t-test.
preprocessing_steps: Import original data from /app/data/FINAL demo open fluency.csv.
Construct latent openness score from available totals (o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo) and median split into low vs high groups.
Clean fluency tokens; form equated node set (responses given by at least 2 participants per group).
Build binary matrices and compute positive Pearson correlation similarity matrices.
Node-wise bootstrap (1000 iterations) retaining 90% of nodes without replacement; compute ASPL for each partial network.
Compare ASPL distributions between groups using Welch t-test; report effect size (Cohen's d).

key_choices.outcome_measure: Average shortest path length (ASPL) from partial semantic networks retaining 90% of nodes.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: Openness group (high vs low) from median split on latent openness composite.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Equate nodes across groups to items produced by at least 2 participants in each group; retain exactly 90% of nodes per bootstrap.
key_choices.missing_data_rule: Impute column means for openness totals when forming composite; ignore missing fluency entries.
key_choices.data_processing: Token cleaning; binary matrices; correlation; graph build; node-wise bootstrap with 90% nodes retained.
key_choices.model_choice: Welch two-sample t-test on ASPL distributions.
key_choices.inference_rule: Two-sided p-value and Cohen's d for ASPL difference.

outcome.name: ASPL
outcome.definition: Average shortest path length computed on partial graphs (90% of nodes retained) using inverse edge-weight distance.

main_predictor.name: openness_group
main_predictor.definition: High vs low groups via median split on latent openness composite from o_ffi, o_bfas, o_neo.

analysis_code.notes: Reads /app/data/FINAL demo open fluency.csv; writes /app/data/task2_results.json. Implements 90% node-wise bootstrap and ASPL comparison. TMFG filtering and small-world model tests are not implemented.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the 90% node-retention rule and compare ASPL. Do not change the model family or dataset.

files.data/analysis_task1__py.py: Python implementation of Task1 pipeline: builds openness groups, constructs semantic networks from verbal fluency, performs 1000 case-wise bootstraps, computes ASPL, CC, Q, and compares groups via Welch t-tests. Outputs task1_results.json.
files.data/analysis_task2__py.py: Python implementation of Task2 pipeline: same preprocessing and grouping; performs 1000 node-wise bootstraps retaining 90% of nodes; computes ASPL and compares groups via Welch t-test; reports Cohen's d. Outputs task2_results.json.

codebase.notes: These Python rewrites approximate the reported R pipelines. Edges are formed from positive Pearson correlations. TMFG filtering and small-world (random vs small-world) tests are not implemented. Modularity uses python-louvain if available; otherwise Q is returned as NaN. All IO is under /app/data.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the original dataset. Run the provided Python entry scripts that implement the planned semantic network analyses. Capture outputs in structured JSON files.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: For Task1: mean ASPL/CC/Q by group, t-statistics, p-values. For Task2: ASPL mean by group, t-statistic, p-value, Cohen's d. Conclusion text on whether the high openness group is more interconnected (lower ASPL), higher CC, and lower Q.
